title: santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. ericoides
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. ericoides, also known as the gray Santolina or woolly-gray Santolina, is a shrubby perennial plant in the sunflower family, native to the Iberian Peninsula and Mediterranean regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. ericoides include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal preparation methods.
